preview_text:
The model favors Senegal at 60.4%, a clear but not dominant margin over Iraq's 12.7% win probability. The gap between favorite and underdog spans nearly 48 percentage points, positioning Senegal as the expected controller of the match on a neutral pitch.

The draw probability of 26.9% reflects meaningful competitive uncertainty in the outcome distribution. Roughly one in four instances see the sides level at full time, suggesting the match is unlikely to be a rout. The spread across all three outcomes—while skewed toward a Senegal victory—indicates Iraq retains a plausible path to an upset or stalemate rather than facing a prohibitive task.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket both price Senegal notably higher than the model does, at 67–69% and 21–21% for draws respectively. The model's win probability for Senegal sits about 6–8 percentage points below market consensus, while both sources agree Iraq's chances hover near 10–12%. This divergence suggests the market perceives Senegal as a stronger favorite than the statistical model; the dashboard does not adjudicate which view is better calibrated.
